Plotting OO-EMG dose-response curve

Lucien Bickerstaff

2026-06-08

# Seed for reproducibility
set.seed(2025)

# Libraries
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(glue)
library(gghalves)
library(patchwork)

source(here("R", "helpers.R"))
source(here("R", "plot_settings.R"))
# Import trial-level EMG outcomes
emg_data <- readRDS(
  here::here("data", "main_data", "emg_outcomes_trial.rds")
)

# Calculate number of participants for each channel
n_data <- emg_data %>%
  group_by(channel) %>%
  summarise(participant_count = n_distinct(participant_id)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct() # Remove duplicates

participant_avg <- emg_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(
    participant_id,
    condition,
    melanopic_edi,
    illuminance,
    channel,
    muscle
  ) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_emg_raw = mean(emg_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_emg_bc  = mean(emg_bc, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_baseline = mean(baseline_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_trials = dplyr::n(),
    n_valid_emg_raw = sum(!is.na(emg_raw)),
    n_valid_emg_bc = sum(!is.na(emg_bc)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

Plotting

We start by creating two separate plots (one for the baseline, 0 lux condition, and one for the non-zero conditions), then we combine then using patchwork

# Create a plot for each channel
for (chan in unique(participant_avg$channel)) {
  # Filter the data for the current channel
  chan <- as.integer(chan)
  muscle <- muscles[chan]
  
  data_channel <- participant_avg %>%
    dplyr::filter(channel == chan)
  
  n = n_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(channel == chan) %>%
    dplyr::pull(participant_count)
  
  # ---- Baseline (condition == 0) plot ----

  baseline_data <- participant_avg %>%
    dplyr::filter(channel == chan, condition == 0)
  
  baseline_plot <- ggplot(
    baseline_data,
    aes(x = melanopic_edi, y = mean_emg_raw)
  ) +
  
    # Half violin for distribution
    geom_half_violin( 
      side = "l", 
      fill = "skyblue", 
      color = NA, 
      alpha = 0.5, 
      adjust = 1, 
      scale  = "count",
      position = position_nudge(x = 0)) +
    
    # Jittered raw points
    geom_jitter(size = 1, color = "grey60", alpha = 0.6, width = 0) + # , position = position_nudge(x = 0.05)
    geom_line(color = "grey60", alpha = 0.1) +
    
    # Boxplot summary
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "white", color = "black") +
  
    # geom_hline(yintercept = 100, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.3) +
  
    # coord_cartesian(
    #   ylim = c(45, 2000),
    #   clip = "off"
    # ) +
    
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = 0,
      labels = "0",
      limits = c(-1, 1),
      expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
  
    scale_y_log10(
      breaks = breaks,
      minor_breaks = minor_breaks
    ) +
  
    labs(
      title = glue("Baseline (dark condition)\nEMG activity ({muscle})\nn={n}"),
      x = "Melanopic EDI (lux)",
      y = "EMG signal (mV)"
    ) +
  
    theme_minimal() +
    
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line()   # draws the single x=0 gridline
    ) +
  
    plot_formatting

  save_rds_safe(
    baseline_plot,
    here("outputs", "figures", "rds", glue("emg_baseline_chan_{chan}.rds"))
  )

  
  # Main plot
  raincould <- ggplot(
    data_channel %>% dplyr::filter(condition != 0), 
    aes(x = melanopic_edi, y = mean_emg_raw)
  ) +
    # Half violin for distribution
    geom_half_violin(
      aes(group=melanopic_edi), 
      side = "l", 
      fill = "skyblue", 
      color = NA, 
      alpha = 0.5, 
      adjust = 1, 
      scale  = "count",
      position = position_nudge(x = 0)) +
    
    # Jittered raw points
    geom_jitter(size = 1, color = "grey60", alpha = 0.6, width = 0) + # , position = position_nudge(x = 0.05)
    geom_line(aes(group = participant_id),
              color = "grey60", alpha = 0.1) +
    
    # Boxplot summary
    geom_boxplot(aes(group=melanopic_edi), width = 0.025, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "white", color = "black") +
    
    # Linear fit line
    geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
    
    # Horizontal reference line at 100
    # geom_hline(yintercept = 100, color = "darkred", alpha = 0.3) +
    
    coord_cartesian(#ylim = c(45, 2000), 
                    xlim = c(300, 10000),
                    clip = "off") +
    scale_x_log10(breaks = breaks, minor_breaks = minor_breaks) +
    scale_y_log10(breaks = breaks, minor_breaks = minor_breaks) +
    
    # Axis labels and theming
    labs(title = glue("EMG activity ({muscle})\nn={n}"),
         x = "Melanopic EDI (lux)",
         y = "EMG signal (mV)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none") +
    plot_formatting
  
  # Save the plot
  save_rds_safe(raincould, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", glue("emg_raincloud_chan_{chan}.rds")))
}

baseline_plot

raincould
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Merge EMG orbicularis oculi baseline + main plots

plot_emg_main <- readRDS(here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "emg_raincloud_chan_2.rds")) +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(0.001, 0.03),
    xlim = c(300, 10000),
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    # Remove y axis from the right plot (keep only on left)
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  ) 
## Coordinate system already present.
## ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
plot_emg_baseline <- readRDS(here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "emg_baseline_chan_2.rds")) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(-0.6, 0.6),
    ylim = c(0.001, 0.03),
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 0,
    labels = "0",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    # Remove x axis title
    axis.title.x = element_blank()
  )
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.
# Make the two panels look like one figure
# Remove the left plot’s right margin and the right plot’s left margin a bit
plot_emg_baseline <- plot_emg_baseline + theme(plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5))
plot_emg_main     <- plot_emg_main     + theme(plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 2))

# Arrow / annotation showing dark condition
# plot_emg_baseline <- plot_emg_baseline +
#   annotate("text",
#            x = 0.25,
#            y = 410,
#            label = "(Dark condition)",
#            colour = "black",
#            fontface = "bold",
#            size = 3,
#            vjust = 0) +
#   annotate("segment",
#            x = 0.25, 
#            xend = 0.1,       # ← makes arrow point left
#            y = 400, 
#            yend = 250,   # position slightly below text
#            colour = "black",
#            arrow = arrow(type = "closed", length = unit(0.15, "cm")))

combined <- 
  plot_emg_baseline + labs(title = "Orbicularis oculi EMG response to light") +
  plot_emg_main + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 5))

print(combined)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Save the plot
formatted_save(here("outputs", "figures", "emg_dose_resp_oo.jpg"), combined, width = 4, height = 4)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
save_rds_safe(combined, here("outputs", "figures", "rds", "emg_dose_resp_oo.rds"))
sessionInfo()
